name: Legal Sea Foods Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 4 quarts littleneck clams (about 1-2/3 cups cooked, chopped)
1 1/2 pounds potatoes, peeled, and diced into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the clams and place them in a large pot with the garlic and water. Steam the clams just until opened, about 6 to 10 minutes depending on their size. Drain and shell the clams, reserving the broth.
2. Mince the clam flesh and set aside.
3. Filter the clam broth through coffee filters or cheesecloth and set aside.
4. In a large, heavy pot, slowly render the salt pork. Remove the cracklings and set them aside.
5. Slowly cook the onions in the fat for about 6 minutes, stirring frequently, until cooked through but not browned.
6. Stir in the flour and cook for 3 minutes.
7. Add the reserved clam broth and Fish Stock, whisking to remove any flour lumps. Bring the liquid to a boil, add the potatoes, lower the heat, and simmer until the potatoes are cooked through, about 15 minutes.
8. Stir in the reserved clams, salt-pork cracklings, and light cream. Heat the chowder until it reaches the desired temperature.
9. Serve the chowder in large soup bowls with oyster crackers on the side.
Serves 8.
